scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings (exceeds 5 minimum), 4+ escalations with clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrail parameters, regulatory compliance, and fallback behavior (plus edge cases surface additional questions totaling well over 5), 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined in escalation #1 and throughout, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific values were invented — all numerical examples are clearly labeled as options/recommendations rather than requirements.

observations.text: Extracted 8 well-reasoned business rules, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum significantly
Demand ambiguity is thoroughly addressed as the first escalation with multiple possible interpretations listed
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 30 with detailed reasoning about why confidence is low
BR-8 (price honoring) and BR-5 (no zero/negative prices) show strong domain reasoning — extracting implied invariants the CEO didn't state
Edge cases section surfaces 7 additional undefined scenarios including regulatory risk, demand flapping, and system outage fallback
No specific values invented — the one example in recommendations ('3× base price') is clearly labeled as a suggestion, not a requirement
BR-7 explicitly marked its testable_assertion as 'UNTESTABLE AS STATED' — honest about specification gaps
Clarifying questions are spread across escalations and edge cases rather than in a single consolidated list, making them slightly harder to count — but they are all present

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context'
